sn47.2:1.1: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Vesālī, in Ambapālī's Grove.
sn47.2:2.2: This is our instruction to you.
sn47.2:2.4: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells contemplating body in body, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and distress with regard to the world;
sn47.2:2.7: he dwells contemplating *dhammas* in *dhammas*, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and distress with regard to the world.
sn47.2:2.8: In this way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is with *sati*.
sn47.2:3.1: And how, *bhikkhus*, is a *bhikkhu* fully aware [*sampajāno*]?
sn47.2:3.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when going forward and returning; acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when looking ahead and looking aside; acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when bending and stretching; acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when carrying the outer robe, bowl, and robe; acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when eating, drinking, chewing, and tasting; acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when urinating and defecating; acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when going, standing, sitting, sleeping, waking, speaking, and keeping silent.
sn47.2:3.3: In this way, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*].
sn47.2:3.4: A *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, should dwell with *sati*, fully aware [*sampajāno*].
sn47.2:3.5: This is our instruction to you.”
